title: Ua system
content:

|affiliation=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="Republic">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' is the second installment, to between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. The latter work establishes that [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] prior to the novel's events, [[Waywa Fybot]]—a [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]] per {{CSWECite|I|300|Fybot, Waywa}}—had left his [[Species/Legends|species']] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and become a [[Police officer/Legends|law enforcement officer]] as part of a tribute that a [[Unidentified treaty|treaty]] dictated the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Star system/Legends|star system]] pay to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government. Since Fybot's departure from the Quor'sav homeworld—established to have been [[Uaua/Legends|Uaua]] by {{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}—must have occurred at some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the aforementioned timeline information, and since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], a tens-of-[[Millennium/Legends|millennia]]–old [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide government, was [[Declaration of a New Order|supplanted]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Ua system must have paid tribute to the Republic.</ref>
}}
{{Quote|He was ''[…]'' conscripted to pay tribute owed by treaty by his system to the central galactic government.|[[Rokur Gepta]], to Lando Calrissian on Waywa Fybot while disguised as Bohhuah Mutdah|<ref name="LCFO" />}}
The '''Ua system''' was a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. It contained the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Uaua/Legends|Uaua]], which was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Bird/Legends|bird]]-like [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. At some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the Ua [[Star system/Legends|system]] became forced by a [[Unidentified treaty|treaty]] to pay tribute to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. As part of the tribute, the Quor'sav [[Waywa Fybot]] was conscripted in a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agency]] and left his home planet, although he would revisit it in a [[Dream/Legends|dream]] [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] later.

==Description==
The Ua [[Star system/Legends|system]]<ref name="CSWE Uaua" /> was a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was situated on the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which linked it to the [[Dilonexa system/Legends|Dilonexa]] and [[Falko system/Legends|Falko systems]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the chief [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Uaua" /> [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] its single [[Star/Legends|star]] was the terrestrial world<ref name="LCFO" /> [[Uaua/Legends|Uaua]].<ref name="CSWE Uaua" />

==History==
[[File:KalFalnlCndros-SecretsOfTheSisarRun.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Quor'sav were native to the planet Uaua in the Ua system.]]
At some point, [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] from other parts of the Centrality,<ref name="Gamer5">{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}}</ref> mostly consisting of individuals of [[Mammal/Legends|mammalian]] [[Species/Legends|species]], accidentally introduced a [[Unidentified virus (Uaua)|virus]] on Uaua<ref name="CSWE Uaua" /> for which mammals served as immune carriers but which was [[Death/Legends|deadly]] to [[Bird/Legends|avian]] species. As a result, the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Quor'sav/Legends|Quor'sav]],<ref name="Gamer5" /> [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] who had evolved from birds,<ref name="LCFO" /> nearly became extinct due to the virus<ref name="CSWE Uaua" /> and became distrustful of non-avian visitors to their homeworld, and any such off-worlders ceased to be welcome on Uaua.<ref name="Gamer5" /> The Quor'sav themselves, however, were already known in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_4.html|Study: Pollution Standards Still Endanger Many}}</ref> by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="HNNdate">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|life/13328_4.html|Study: Pollution Standards Still Endanger Many}} is set on the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 13:3:28, which corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> at which point members of the species resided on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="HNN" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TEA" />

At some point, a [[Unidentified treaty|treaty]] was signed which forced the Ua system to pay tribute to<ref name="LCFO" /> the Republic.<ref name="Republic" /> As part of the tribute,<ref name="LCFO" /> the Quor'sav<ref name="CSWE Fybot">{{CSWECite|I|300|Fybot, Waywa}}</ref> [[Waywa Fybot]] was conscripted in a [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement agency]] and left his homeworld<ref name="LCFO" /> at some point by 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="Republic" /> In the view of Fybot, who eventually came to work as an undercover [[Spice/Legends|narcotics]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] in service to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], his enjoyment of life diminished once he had left Uaua.<ref name="LCFO" />

Sometime between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="LCFOdate">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy, of which ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' is the second installment, to between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> Fybot was traveling through the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] aboard [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], during the local phenomenon known as the [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]] in order to arrest the trillionaire [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]]. The Flamewind affected the perception and behavior of beings not protected by adequate shielding, with Fybot experiencing a [[Dream/Legends|dream]] in which he was transported back to Uaua. In the dream, the Quor'sav visited his [[Waywa Fybot's home|former home]] on the planet.<ref name="LCFO" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:A_Campaign_Guide_to_the_Centrality_G5.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Michael Kogge named the Ua system for the article "A Campaign Guide to the Centrality."]]
The Ua system was introduced in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]," which was authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer5" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref> The article, which built on the<ref name="Gamer5" /> [[1983]]<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]] and detailed the Centrality sector that was featured in that series, described the system's planet Uaua as the homeworld of the avian Quor'sav species.<ref name="Gamer5" />

It was not until the publication of [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', however, that Waywa Fybot,<ref name="CSWE Fybot" /> an avian character introduced in the second entry of Smith's trilogy, ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'',<ref name="LCFO" /> was established to have been a Quor'sav.<ref name="CSWE Fybot" /> Thereby, the passage from ''Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon'' in which Fybot visits his species' homeworld in a dream sequence was [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] established as the first appearance of the Ua system.<ref name="LCFO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the system in grid square U-8.<ref name="TEA" />
